title: lora_l2.c:284 ssize_t hwid_len compared to size_t without cast
description:
In generate_eui64() at line 284:
```c
if ((size_t)hwid_len > sizeof(hwid)) {
```

The hwid_len variable is ssize_t. At line 269, it's assigned from hwinfo_get_device_id() which returns ssize_t (negative on error). Lines 270-277 check for hwid_len < 0, and line 278-283 checks for hwid_len == 0.

By line 284, hwid_len is known to be positive, so the cast to size_t is safe. However, hwinfo_get_device_id() could theoretically return a value larger than INT_MAX on some exotic platform, though this is extremely unlikely for a device ID (typically 4-32 bytes).

SEVERITY: P4 - no practical bug on embedded targets where sizeof(hwid) is 32 bytes.

NO ACTION NEEDED - the check is defensive and correct.
